text: Using HTTPS is the first step to securing a website. This protocol encrypts data between the user and the server, preventing hackers from stealing sensitive information like passwords or credit card numbers. Authentication systems, such as requiring strong passwords or two-factor verification, ensure that only authorized users can access private areas. Input validation is also crucial—websites must check and clean any data users submit, like in search boxes or forms. Without this, attackers can inject malicious code, leading to common vulnerabilities like SQL injection or cross-site scripting. By following these practices, developers can build safer, more reliable web applications.
